abstract: Comprehensive guide for authoring git commit messages following the Conventional Commits specification. Covers commit types (feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, build, ci, chore, revert), imperative mood formatting, breaking change notation with ! or BREAKING CHANGE footer, scope usage, multi-line body and footer conventions, AI attribution with Assisted-by footer, and the --signoff git flag requirement for DCO compliance. Includes quick reference table mapping commit types to semantic version bumps, real-world examples, common mistakes to avoid, and a pre-commit verification checklist.
